mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short term as investors anticipate continued economic strength.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 4.0% for a continuous 12-month period, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation above a certain threshold signals potential central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3.0% for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive (i.e., > 55% positive instances).

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with accommodative monetary policy (low federal funds rate) creates an environment of strong consumer demand and cheap financing, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in higher future profits.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of such triggered instances over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates weakening manufacturing activity and potential economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as profit expectations decline and recession fears grow.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month annualized growth falls below 0.5% during a period identified as a late-cycle expansion, but S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts reflect robust consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and employment growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate stronger corporate earnings and a healthy economy.
failure_condition: If HOUST consistently exceeds 1200.0k units for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are predominantly negative (i.e., < 45% positive instances) over that period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong payroll employment growth signals a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economic outlook improves.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently adds more than 150k jobs per month for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat for more than 3 consecutive months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate, especially when it crosses a certain threshold, indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation or overheating, which typically leads to higher borrowing costs and reduced corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5% and shows a 3-month increase of over 0.25%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive (i.e., > 60% positive instances) over a 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with low unemployment indicates a robust and efficient economy, signaling strong corporate earnings potential and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as the overall economic health supports equity valuations.
failure_condition: If both GDP growth and unemployment conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.
